comments.author: backlog
comments.text: Verified in current checkout. extensions/tau/src/agents-menu/state.ts:125-135 returns null from readSettingsFile(...) both for ENOENT and for all other errors, including JSON parse failures and non-object content (JSON.parse succeeds, then isRecord(parsed) ? parsed : null). activate(...) at lines 282-300 treats that null as missing settings: readEnabledAgentsFromSettings(null) and readRalphEnabledAgentsFromSettings(null) both return undefined, and buildStateFromEnabledAgents(...) with enabledAgents === undefined produces no disabled agents (lines 96-107). So corrupted or structurally invalid .pi/settings.json silently falls back to 'everything enabled' semantics for agent selection. This is a real fail-open behavior in policy/config handling.

comments.author: backlog
comments.text: Implemented fail-closed behavior in `extensions/tau/src/agents-menu/state.ts`: `readSettingsFile` now returns tagged read results (`missing` | `valid` | `invalid`) so invalid settings (JSON parse errors or non-object JSON) are distinguished from missing files. `AgentSelectionStore.activate` now immediately fails closed on `invalid` by disabling all available agents for the project state, instead of treating invalid settings as missing. Added regression coverage in `extensions/tau/test/agents-settings.test.ts` for malformed JSON and non-object JSON to assert all agents are disabled.
